intent: Design a globally available internal agent workflow-template authoring tool and its shared validation, persistence, desktop-server, stale-write, field-guide, permission, and verification boundaries.
must_preserve: Direct validated writes for user-template create/update; no mandatory preview step.
Global ordinary-chat availability with active workflow phase-policy and permission gating.
Builtin templates are read-only and modified only through copy-then-edit.
Delete only uniquely identified user templates and treat delete as destructive.
Update/delete stale-write rejection; create current id conflict checks.
Field guide must be discoverable and validation-aligned.
Settings Workflows compatibility through the same persisted registry/store after reload/refetch.
Template edits affect future workflow sessions only.
No protected non-workflow user state mutation.
allowed_optimization_scope: Refactor shared workflow template validation to remove drift between registry, API, and tool.
Add a small authoring service abstraction when it centralizes operation orchestration and stale-write checks.
Add local desktop/server authoring route for cache-owning process writes.
Avoid desktop UI changes unless implementation proves they are necessary for compatibility.
acceptance_obligations: Agent can guide, validate, create, update, duplicate, and delete workflow templates through the tool.
Valid create/update writes persist directly to the existing workflow registry store.
Settings Workflows can show agent-authored templates after reload/refetch.
Builtin edit requests copy to a user template first.
Stale update/delete and all invalid/denied/ambiguous operations leave config unchanged.
Tool results are transcript-auditable and deterministic enough for tests.

operations.proof: create success and conflict no-write tests
fresh update and stale rejection tests
builtin copy naming and conflict tests
delete destructive metadata and no-write rejection tests

capability_preservation.surface_minimization_policy: A single operation-discriminated tool is preferred, but command-surface minimization must not delete capability; remap preserved operations to an explicit entry point or record a user-confirmed deferral.

mp_obligations.decision: Use local desktop/server authoring endpoint when CC_JIANGXIA_DESKTOP_SERVER_URL is configured.
Use a colocated static guide plus drift tests for first implementation.

implementation_target.target_project_cognition_status: fresh/query_ready during plan intake; advisory only

target_evidence_status: project cognition query plus live source reads
boundary_constraints: Do not write protected non-workflow state.
Do not mutate active or historical workflow session snapshots.
Do not directly mutate builtin templates.
Route desktop-launched writes through the local server when configured.

consequence_gate.trigger_reason: Feature mutates user workflow config, affects future workflow execution, has destructive delete behavior, and crosses tool/server/desktop/runtime compatibility surfaces.

state_behavior_matrix.behavior: list returns builtins; valid create may create config through registry service
write operations reject and leave file unchanged
copy builtin to user template before modification
reject with persisted false and inspect-and-retry

dependency_impact: Shared validation affects registry, API, and tool; tests must prove parity.
Desktop server authoring endpoint affects local server API tests and tool routing tests.
Phase policy affects mutating tool availability during workflow sessions.
recovery_and_validation: No-write rejection for invalid, stale, denied, ambiguous, builtin-protected, or server-unavailable paths.
Tool result includes operation, persisted flag, diagnostics, and next action.

coverage_gaps.gap: Exact implementation endpoint shape may change if tasks choose to extend existing endpoints instead.
coverage_gaps.routing_decision: continue with preferred authoring endpoint contract

consequence_obligations.claim: Valid create/update writes directly and rejected operations do not write.
Only workflow template config is mutated and unknown fields are preserved.
Validation semantics are shared with registry/API.
Template edits do not mutate session snapshots.
Global tool respects active phase policy.
Builtin copy naming uses confirmed defaults and suffixes.
Field guide is discoverable and validation-aligned.

operational_consequence_decisions: Use SHA-256 basis hashes for update/delete stale-write protection.
Use local desktop/server authoring endpoint when CC_JIANGXIA_DESKTOP_SERVER_URL is present.
Use static colocated field guide with drift tests for first implementation.
Use operation-level policy gate rather than whole-tool disallow list.
stop_and_reopen_conditions: Mandatory preview-only persistence is reintroduced for valid user-template create/update.
Direct builtin mutation, deletion, or builtin id shadowing is proposed.
Implementation writes outside the workflow template config/registry service.
Template edit mutates active or historical workflow session snapshots.
Global authoring tool bypasses active workflow phase action policy.
Field guide cannot be tested against validation concepts.
Unsupported non-linear workflow shapes are accepted without explicit product decision.
